name: Social Graph / Contacts Import
definition_origin: Contacts-import-as-onboarding-step pioneered by Path 2011 (asked Day 0, infamous 2012 FTC settlement $800K for harvesting addressbooks without consent), Snapchat early days (2012-2013), Instagram (2010+), WhatsApp (2009+), Twitter (Find Friends 2007+), LinkedIn (Find Connections 2003+ — defined the pattern). Codified across 2018-2024 by privacy-regulatory pressure (GDPR 2018, CCPA 2020, COPPA amendments 2025) and Apple iOS 14 transparency push. The 2024-2026 doctrine has INVERTED — defer contacts-import entirely until VALUE FELT, not Day 0. Sources: Apple HIG Contacts permissions; Apple Privacy Manifest 2024 (NSContactsUsageDescription scrutinized); Path FTC settlement 2013; Snap Privacy Center; LinkedIn data-scraping settlements; multiple privacy-class-actions 2018-2024 against Day-0 contacts harvesting. Cross-references item 24 (permission priming pattern) and item 65 (pre-permission soft-ask screen).
core_mechanism: Contacts permission grants the app full access to the device addressbook (names, phone numbers, emails, photos, custom labels). Useful for: (a) friend-finding for social apps (find people you know on the app), (b) viral-loop expansion (invite contacts not yet on app), (c) caller-ID style features (Truecaller). The DAY-0 ASK pattern — asking immediately on first launch — generates short-term virality but tanks trust + privacy regulator attention. The DEFER-UNTIL-VALUE-FELT pattern (post-2024 best practice) waits until user has experienced core value, then prompts in a value-aligned context ('Find friends to share with?' AFTER first post created). Five reinforcing mechanisms for the deferred approach: (1) Trust calibration — user has seen value before being asked for sensitive permission. (2) Regulatory compliance — GDPR Art. 6 lawful basis is stronger when contextual (legitimate interest + value alignment) than cold (purpose-creep). (3) Higher opt-in — soft-ask after value > cold soft-ask Day 0 by 2-3×. (4) Reduced uninstall risk — Day-0 contacts ask is the #1 onboarding-uninstall trigger per multiple Mobbin teardowns. (5) FTC / EU enforcement avoidance — Day-0 contacts harvesting was the explicit Path 2013 FTC violation; precedent stands.
neuroscience_correlate: Privacy-as-cognitive-cost (Acquisti 2015 'Behavioral Economics of Privacy'). Trust calibration via affective forecasting (Wilson & Gilbert 2003) — user predicts future regret if they grant permission cold. Reactance (Brehm 1966) — feeling coerced into sharing contacts triggers psychological resistance.
empirical_evidence: Path 2013 FTC settlement: $800K + 20-year audit obligation for collecting addressbooks of millions of users without consent (the foundational privacy-violation precedent for contacts harvesting). Snap settled California AG 2020 over similar issues. LinkedIn class action 2017 ($13M) over phantom-friend invites. Instagram contacts-import opt-in rate: 35-45% Day-0 (ask immediately) vs 60-75% deferred-until-first-action (anecdotal benchmarks across 2020-2024 teardowns). WhatsApp Day-0 contacts ask is exceptional — opt-in 90%+ but only because category value (messaging your contacts) is universally understood; this is NOT generalizable. iOS 14 (2020) Contacts permission tightened — 'Limited Contacts Access' option added 2023 — opt-in dropped further with the limited option's ambiguity. Apple privacy nutrition labels (2020+) and privacy-manifest 2024 require explicit declaration — apps that harvest contacts without listing it face App Store rejection. Mobbin 2024 teardowns: post-2022 social-app onboarding overwhelmingly defers contacts ask until post-value moment. RevenueCat 2025 doesn't track contacts-permission specifically but social-category D7 retention dropped 2018-2022 alongside Day-0 contacts-permission decline. EU GDPR enforcement: multiple decisions 2019-2024 against contacts-harvesting without explicit consent (Italy DPA against TikTok 2023 €15M for inadequate consent flow).
measurable_outcome_metric: Contacts-permission opt-in rate (target ≥60% if deferred-until-value, ≥35% if Day-0 — both lower than push or photos). Time-to-permission-ask (deferred metric — earlier = lower opt-in + higher uninstall risk). Friend-discovery success rate (% of users with contacts who find ≥1 connection — drives social-app activation). Contacts-permission-denied retention (D7) vs granted — should be SIMILAR (if denial tanks retention, app is over-reliant on contacts feature). Uninstall rate within 24h of contacts ask (Day-0 ask correlates with 5-15% uninstall spike).
conversion_lift_benchmark: Deferred-until-value-felt: 2-3× opt-in vs Day-0 cold (60-75% vs 35-45%, anecdotal teardown benchmarks). Day-0 contacts ask: 5-15% uninstall spike within 24h (multiple teardown reports). Path 2013 FTC: $800K settlement (cautionary, not a lift).
engineering_tactics: DEFAULT PATTERN: Defer contacts ask entirely until value felt. Trigger contextually after user completes core action (first post, first chat, first feature use). NEVER ask Day 0 except in WhatsApp-tier categories where value is universally understood. SEQUENCE: (1) Skip contacts during onboarding entirely — onboarding flow does not include contacts step. (2) After user completes core action, surface a contextual permission-priming screen (item 65 pattern) — bottom-sheet or modal. Headline: VALUE-PROP tied to action just completed. Example: 'Share with [contacts who are also on App]?' AFTER first post. (3) Two buttons: PRIMARY 'Find friends' (triggers native dialog) — SECONDARY 'Maybe later' (defers; re-prompt at next contextual moment, max 2-3 times). (4) iOS native dialog: requestAccess(for: .contacts) via CNContactStore. NSContactsUsageDescription required in Info.plist with specific value-prop copy ('Find your friends who are already on [App]'). (5) Android: READ_CONTACTS permission via runtime grant (M+). (6) ON GRANT: server-side hash contact phone numbers + emails (SHA-256 + per-user salt) and match against existing user phone-hashes. NEVER store raw contact data server-side. iOS Contacts limited-access mode: handle gracefully — user may grant only specific contacts. (7) FRIEND-DISCOVERY UI: show matched friends as in-app surface (not push-spam). 'Add' button per match — explicit consent for friend-add. Forbid: auto-following, auto-friending, auto-spamming non-users with invites. (8) NON-USER INVITE PATH: explicit user-initiated invite only — 'Invite [contact]' button per non-user. Forbid: bulk-invite-all, phantom-invite (sending as user without their explicit per-invite consent — LinkedIn 2017 $13M precedent). (9) PRIVACY MANIFEST 2024: declare ContactsList in PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy with NSPrivacyAccessedAPIType + reasons. (10) DELETION: provide user-accessible 'Disconnect contacts' setting that deletes all hashed contact data server-side (GDPR Article 17 right to erasure).
real_world_examples: (1) Instagram (post-2022 doctrine): contacts ask deferred until first feed view; 'Find friends from contacts' surface in Profile tab as user-initiated. (2) TikTok (post-2023 doctrine, after Italy DPA €15M settlement): contacts ask deferred and explicit purpose disclosure. (3) Snapchat: contacts deferred to first chat moment; explicit purpose. (4) Twitter (now X): 'Find People' is user-initiated in Settings, not Day 0. (5) WhatsApp (exception — Day-0 contacts ask): opt-in 90%+ because category value is universally understood. (6) LinkedIn: 'Add Connections' user-initiated; post-2017 settlement uses explicit consent per invite. (7) Hinge / Bumble / Tinder (dating): defer entirely or skip — don't want users matching with their contacts. (8) BeReal: deferred — friends added via username, not contacts harvesting. (9) Marco Polo (video messaging): deferred contacts ask after first video sent. (10) Truecaller (caller-ID category): Day-0 contacts ask justified by category value, but hit by EU regulatory action; reduced footprint 2024+. (11) Path 2011-2013: the cautionary-tale Day-0 contacts harvest, FTC settlement, eventual app shutdown. (12) Clubhouse 2021: aggressive Day-0 contacts ask drove rapid viral growth + privacy backlash.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-specific: iOS NSContactsUsageDescription + Android READ_CONTACTS runtime permission. iOS Contacts limited-access (2023+) — user grants specific contacts only. Web has no addressbook API (privacy by design). Web-equivalent: user uploads CSV (file picker) or pastes emails — explicit consent per address. Web social-graph imports via Google Contacts OAuth (granular permission scope) or LinkedIn OAuth — different consent model.
platform_design_language_alignment: Pre-permission soft-ask screen aligns with Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) bottom-sheet and M3E large bottom-sheet (Android 16+). Native iOS/Android contacts permission dialog is OS-controlled. App should prepare user with branded screen first.
category_fit: Strongest fit: social networks (Instagram, Snapchat, BeReal — but DEFERRED), messaging (WhatsApp — Day-0 acceptable as exception, Signal, Telegram, Marco Polo), dating (Hinge — DEFER OR SKIP), professional networks (LinkedIn — user-initiated only post-2017 settlement). LOWER FIT: utility / productivity (no social-graph value), wellness / habit (privacy-first reigns), fintech (contacts-import for P2P payments — Cash App, Venmo — ASK CONTEXTUALLY at first-transfer-to-non-existing-user, NOT Day 0). EXCLUSIONS: any app where contacts-import value is not category-table-stakes.
solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week for deferred contextual contacts-ask + friend-discovery surface + hash-based matching backend. Solo-dev should DEFAULT to skipping contacts entirely unless app is genuinely social-graph-driven; Day-0 contacts ask is regulator + uninstall + trust risk. Dependencies: iOS Contacts framework (CNContactStore), Android ContactsContract API, server-side hashing infrastructure (Supabase Edge Functions), privacy manifest declaration.
tooling_options: iOS: Contacts framework (CNContactStore + CNContactFetchRequest). Android: ContactsContract API. Hashing: SHA-256 with per-user salt; libs: Apple CryptoKit (iOS), java.security.MessageDigest (Android). Server-side: Supabase Postgres + Edge Functions for matching; Firestore + Cloud Functions; Convex; Hasura. Privacy / compliance: privacy manifest tooling (Apple's PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy), Truvy / Termly privacy-policy generators for contacts-disclosure copy. NO MAGIC TOOL — DIY for compliance reasons (avoid 3rd-party SDKs that harvest contacts opaquely).
claude_design_application: Prompt fragment: 'Build deferred contacts-import pattern for social-graph features. (1) NEVER ask contacts permission during onboarding. NEVER cold-launch native dialog. (2) Trigger contextually after user completes core action — for social app, AFTER first post created; for messaging, AFTER first chat sent; for fintech P2P, AFTER first-transfer-to-existing-user before transfer-to-non-existing-user. (3) Show pre-permission soft-ask screen (item 65 pattern) — bottom-sheet or modal with VALUE-PROP headline tied to action just completed (e.g., "Find friends already on [App]?"). Two buttons: PRIMARY "Find friends" (triggers CNContactStore.requestAccess on iOS, ActivityCompat.requestPermissions(READ_CONTACTS) on Android), SECONDARY "Maybe later" (defers; re-prompt at next contextual moment, max 2-3 times). (4) NSContactsUsageDescription with specific value-prop copy in Info.plist ("Find your friends who are already on [App]"). (5) On grant: server-side hash contact phone numbers + emails (SHA-256 + per-user salt) and match against existing user phone-hashes. NEVER store raw contact data server-side. (6) Friend-discovery UI: matched friends shown as in-app surface (not push-spam). "Add" button per match — explicit consent. (7) Non-user invite: user-initiated only, per-invite explicit consent. NO bulk-invite-all, NO phantom-invite (LinkedIn 2017 $13M precedent). (8) Privacy manifest declaration: ContactsList in PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy with NSPrivacyAccessedAPIType + reasons. (9) Disconnect-contacts setting in user Settings — deletes all hashed contact data server-side (GDPR Article 17). Forbid: Day-0 contacts ask (Path 2013 FTC precedent), bulk-invite, phantom-invite, raw-contact server storage, indigo-500 button on permission-priming screen, ✨ emoji on "Find friends" copy.' Second fragment: 'Build the contextual permission-priming bottom-sheet — Liquid Glass material on iOS 26+. Headline tied to user''s just-completed action. Two buttons. NO marketing language about "connecting your network" — straightforward, Sage register.'
stitch_application: Prompt fragment: 'Mockup the deferred contacts-import pre-permission screen. Bottom-sheet design. Top 30%: simple custom illustration of two-people-icon (NOT Lucide UserPlus + Users + Heart trinity), monochrome. Headline 28pt Cabinet Grotesk weight 400 "Find friends already on [App]". Subhead 14pt Söhne neutral-grey "We''ll match your contacts to find people you know. Your contacts stay private — we hash them on your device.". Middle 30%: 3-bullet vertical list (NOT three-column grid), custom-numbered: "01. Find people you know", "02. Contacts stay private", "03. Disconnect anytime". Bottom 30%: primary CTA "Find friends" 56pt height, solid ember-orange (NOT gradient), ink-color text. Below: secondary text-link "Maybe later" centered, 14pt ink/60. Background: bone #F4EFE6 with subtle ink/5 surface tint. Forbid: ✨🔒💌👥 emoji, Lucide icon trinity, indigo-500 button, purple-blue gradient, Inter font, three-column feature grid, hover:scale-105, rounded-xl on every element, marketing-language about "network growth". Aesthetic: Sage register, restrained, privacy-respectful tone. The screen comes AFTER user has completed core action (first post / chat / transfer) — NOT during onboarding.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI typically (1) places contacts-ask in the onboarding flow at Day 0 (canonical antipattern; Path 2013 precedent), (2) writes overly-aggressive 'Find your network ✨' Lifestyle copy instead of Sage privacy-respectful, (3) defaults to indigo-500 + purple-blue gradient on the permission screen (slop), (4) implements bulk-invite-all default (LinkedIn 2017 $13M precedent), (5) stores raw contact data server-side (GDPR Article 9 + cardinal violation), (6) skips per-user salting on phone-hash (allows rainbow-table attack), (7) skips privacy manifest declaration (App Store rejection 2024+), (8) skips disconnect-contacts setting (GDPR Article 17 violation). Counter-prompt: 'Build deferred contacts-import — DO NOT include in onboarding flow. Trigger contextually after first core-action completed. Pre-permission soft-ask screen with VALUE-PROP tied to action just completed. Sage register, NOT Magician/Lifestyle. Server-side: SHA-256 hash with per-user salt; NEVER store raw contact data. Friend-discovery as in-app surface, NOT push-spam. Non-user invite is user-initiated per-invite, explicit consent — NEVER bulk-invite-all, NEVER phantom-invite (LinkedIn 2017 $13M precedent). Privacy manifest declaration. Disconnect-contacts setting. Forbid: Day-0 ask, indigo-500 + purple-blue gradient, ✨ emoji, three-column feature grid, Inter font, raw-contact server storage.'
anti_patterns: (1) Day-0 contacts-import ask (Path 2013 FTC $800K precedent; Italy DPA TikTok €15M precedent). (2) Bulk-invite-all defaults (LinkedIn 2017 $13M class action precedent). (3) Phantom-invite (sending as user without explicit per-invite consent — LinkedIn precedent). (4) Raw contact data server-side storage (GDPR Article 9 + Path FTC violation). (5) Skipping per-user salting on phone-hash (rainbow-table vulnerability). (6) Auto-following / auto-friending matched contacts (consent violation). (7) Spam-bombing non-users with invites (CAN-SPAM, GDPR ePrivacy Directive). (8) Hidden disconnect-contacts setting (GDPR Article 17 violation). (9) iOS limited-access mode not handled gracefully (UX failure). (10) NSContactsUsageDescription with vague copy ('Find friends' insufficient — must be specific). (11) Skipping privacy manifest declaration in PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy (Apple rejection 2024+). (12) Indigo-500 + purple-blue gradient + ✨ emoji slop on permission screen.
regulatory_risk: HIGH if mishandled. (a) GDPR Article 6 (lawful basis), Article 9 (special category for inferred data), Article 17 (right to erasure), Article 13 (transparency). (b) Path 2013 FTC $800K + 20-year audit obligation. (c) Italy DPA TikTok 2023 €15M for inadequate consent. (d) LinkedIn 2017 $13M phantom-invite class action. (e) CCPA / CPRA — contacts as personal info. (f) COPPA — under-13 contacts harvesting requires VPC. (g) CAN-SPAM — contacts-spam invites. (h) ePrivacy Directive — strict consent for marketing. (i) FTC Section 5 — deceptive consent flows. (j) Apple Privacy Manifest 2024 — declaration mandatory; non-compliance = App Store rejection. (k) EU Digital Fairness Act (Q4 2026) — addressbook harvesting under specific scrutiny.
platform_constraints_2026: Apple App Store Guideline 5.1.1 — privacy policy + data-minimization mandatory. Apple Guideline 5.1.2 — limit data collection to specific declared purposes. Apple Privacy Manifest 2024 (PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy) — must declare ContactsList in NSPrivacyAccessedAPIType with reason codes. NSContactsUsageDescription mandatory in Info.plist. iOS Contacts limited-access mode (2023+) requires graceful handling. Android 14+ partial-access for media (similar concept may extend to contacts). Android READ_CONTACTS runtime permission. Apple Privacy Nutrition Labels (2020+) require explicit data-collection disclosure. EU GDPR + ePrivacy Directive. UK ICO guidance on contacts-import. CA CPRA + state-level laws.
accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2 — pre-permission screen must be screen-reader accessible. Touch target 24×24 px (SC 2.5.8) for buttons. Color contrast 4.5:1 (SC 1.4.3). Sufficient time (SC 2.2.1). Native contacts dialog OS-controlled. Manual contact-add fallback for users who can't or won't grant permission — friend-by-username OR invite-by-phone-number. EU EAA 2025 enforcement.
pitfalls_caveats: (1) Day-0 contacts ask = uninstall spike + regulator attention; defer always. (2) iOS limited-access mode requires graceful handling — 'Limited' returns subset of contacts; UI should not break. (3) Phone-number normalization across countries (E.164 format required for hashing match). (4) Email-vs-phone matching — typically only one is reliable per user. (5) Per-user salt on hashing prevents cross-user-table joins (privacy uplift). (6) Privacy manifest declaration is granular — wrong reason code = rejection. (7) Disconnect-contacts must delete server-side hashes too (not just unlink). (8) International phone-format quirks (Japan local format, US 10-digit). (9) Limited-access mode means re-prompting permission re-prompts the limited UI, not the full dialog (iOS quirk). (10) Cross-platform parity (iOS limited-access has no Android equivalent currently). (11) Avoid 3rd-party SDKs that harvest contacts opaquely (e.g., older Pinpoint, AppsFlyer some configurations) — compliance liability. (12) WhatsApp exception is historical; new apps cannot rely on Day-0 contacts ask getting same opt-in.
cross_cultural_validity: EU strict — GDPR + ePrivacy Directive + member-state-specific (Italy, Germany, France stricter). LATAM: phone OTP via WhatsApp + addressbook commonly intertwined. India: WhatsApp dominant; addressbook integration cultural norm. China: super-app context (WeChat addressbook integrated); foreign apps face firewall. JP: privacy-conscious; deferred ask + clear copy required. KR: similar to JP. MENA: addressbook + WhatsApp common; privacy norms vary by country. RTL Arabic: addressbook UI RTL.
prescribed_action: DEFER contacts-import entirely from onboarding. Trigger contextually AFTER user completes core action (first post / chat / transfer / feature use). Pre-permission soft-ask screen (item 65 pattern) with VALUE-PROP headline tied to action just completed. Native iOS/Android dialog only after Allow on soft-ask. Specific NSContactsUsageDescription copy. Server-side: SHA-256 hash with per-user salt; NEVER store raw contacts. Friend-discovery as in-app surface, not push-spam. Non-user invite is user-initiated per-invite, explicit consent. Forbid: bulk-invite-all (LinkedIn 2017 $13M precedent), phantom-invite, raw-contact server storage. Privacy manifest declaration mandatory (Apple 2024+). Disconnect-contacts setting (GDPR Article 17). iOS limited-access mode graceful handling. Day-0 contacts ask is OUT OF DOCTRINE 2024-2026 (Path 2013 + Italy TikTok 2023 precedents). WhatsApp-tier exceptions only when category value is universally understood AND legitimate-interest can be defended.
questionnaire_relevance: N/A — contacts-import is post-onboarding. Onboarding does not include contacts step.
aha_relevance: Contacts-import IS NOT the aha. In social apps, the aha is FIRST POST / FIRST CHAT / FIRST DM. Contacts-import comes AFTER as expansion mechanic. In viral-network-effect apps, the secondary aha is finding-someone-you-know — but that aha must come AFTER first-action, NOT as gating step.
telemetry_events: contacts_permission_screen_viewed (post first-action context), contacts_permission_allowed, contacts_permission_deferred, contacts_native_dialog_shown, contacts_native_dialog_allowed, contacts_native_dialog_denied, contacts_limited_access_granted (iOS 14+), contacts_hashed_uploaded, contacts_match_found, contact_friend_added, contact_invite_sent, contacts_disconnected (right-to-erasure event).
measurement_horizon: Day 7 (% with contacts permission granted); Day 30 (friend-add count from matched contacts).
emotional_archetype_alignment: PRIMARY: Sage (privacy-respectful, transparent — 'we hash on device', 'disconnect anytime'). SECONDARY: Caregiver (concern for user privacy). MISMATCHES: Magician (theatrical 'find your network ✨' is unprofessional in privacy-context), Outlaw (rebellion clashes with consent context), Jester (humor undermines privacy gravitas).
anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against Day-0 Cold Contacts Ask (canonical Path 2013 antipattern), Bulk-Invite-All, Phantom-Invite (LinkedIn 2017), Raw-Contact Server Storage, Cold Contextless Permission. Pairs WITH item 24 (permission priming pattern), item 65 (soft-ask screen), item 58 (push priming for similar deferred pattern).
